IPartyPool","name":"pool","type":"address"}],"name":"working","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"kind":"dev","methods":{"burnAmounts(address,uint256)":{"params":{"lpTokenAmount":"The amount of LP tokens to burn"},"returns":{"withdrawAmounts":"Array of token amounts that will be received"}},"burnSwapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"lpAmount":"amount of LP tokens to burn","outputTokenIndex":"index of target asset to receive"}},"denominators(address)":{"details":"Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`.         Equivalent to `pool.denominators()` in the previous interface; moved here so that         PartyPool's deployed bytecode stays within EIP-170."},"fees(address)":{"details":"Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`."},"fetchPoolState(address)":{"details":"Same read-only-reentrancy caveat as the other quote helpers \u2014 do not use as      a same-transaction price oracle (see the interface-level NatSpec)."},"maxLpForBudget(address,uint256,uint256)":{"returns":{"amountInUsed":"  total uint input that will be transferred at that lpAmountOut","inFee":"         swap-leg fee portion of amountInUsed","lpAmountOut":"   largest feasible LP amount; 0 if budget is too small to mint anything"}},"mintAmounts(address,uint256)":{"details":"Returns minimum token amounts (rounded up) to receive `lpTokenAmount` LP tokens      at current pool proportions. Returns zeros for the initial deposit (handled by      transferring tokens first, then calling `initialMint()`).","params":{"lpTokenAmount":"The desired amount of LP tokens"},"returns":{"depositAmounts":"Array of token amounts to deposit (rounded up)"}},"poolPrice(address,uint256)":{"details":"Balanced approximation: pool value \u2248 nAssets \u00d7 quoteBalance.      Per-LP value = nAssets \u00d7 quoteBalance / totalSupply / D[quote].","params":{"quoteTokenIndex":"index of the quote asset in which to denominate the LP price"},"returns":{"_0":"price Q64.64 value equal to quote per LP token unit"}},"price(address,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Computed as `exp((q[input] \u2212 q[output]) / b) \u00d7 D[input] / D[output]` where      `b = \u03ba \u00b7 effectiveSigmaQ` and `D[k] = denominators(pool)[k]`.      Anchored to the pool's `effectiveSigmaQ` (the block-aligned `min(\u03c3_swap, \u03c3_live)`      that swap()/swapMint()/burn() price against), so this is the *executable* marginal      price, not a live-\u03a3q theoretical price. This is what makes the exact-price ceiling      workflow in `swapAmountsForExactPrice` safe: both share the same execution anchor.      Represents the cost in input token units to acquire one unit of the output token.      Fee-free and infinitesimal \u2014 actual cost for a finite swap will be higher.      On a balanced pool with equal denominators this returns exactly `1 << 128`.","params":{"inputTokenIndex":"index of the token being sold","outputTokenIndex":"index of the token being bought"},"returns":{"_0":"External buy price as Q128.128 uint256"}},"swapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"inputTokenIndex":"index of token being sold","maxAmountIn":"exact input to transfer (fee is deducted from output, not added to input)","outputTokenIndex":"index of token being bought","pool":"pool being quoted"},"returns":{"amountIn":" exact input transferred (= maxAmountIn)","amountOut":"net output amount user would receive (gross output minus outFee)","outFee":"   fee deducted from gross output"}},"swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Given desired NET output `amountOut` (what the caller receives after fee), returns the      required input without iteration. Reverts if `amountOut` is infeasible.","params":{"amountOut":"desired NET output in uint token units (after fee deduction)","inputTokenIndex":"index of token being sold","outputTokenIndex":"index of token being bought"},"returns":{"amountIn":"       total uint input required (no fee on input; fee is on output side)","outFee":"         fee deducted from gross output"}},"swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Uses 64 iterations of bisection on the actual two-sided LMSR state. Returns      `(amountIn, minAmountOut, fee)` \u2014 pass both directly to `pool.swap()`.      NOT intended to be called from on-chain operations.      Caller workflow:        uint256 target = info.price(pool, i, j) * 1005 / 1000; // 0.5% slippage ceiling        (uint256 maxIn, uint256 minOut,) = info.swapAmountsForExactPrice(pool, i, j, target);        pool.swap(payer, sel, recv, i, j, maxIn, minOut, deadline, false, \"\");","params":{"inputTokenIndex":"index of token being sold","maxPrice":"Q128.128 denomination-adjusted ceiling on P_fwd.                         Must be strictly greater than info.price(pool, i, j).","outputTokenIndex":"index of token being bought"},"returns":{"amountIn":" exact input (uint token units; fee is on output side)","amountOut":"net output (uint token units); pass as `minAmountOut` to `swap()`","outFee":"   fee deducted from gross output"}},"swapMintAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"params":{"inputTokenIndex":"index of the input token","lpAmountOut":"target LP shares the caller wants minted"},"returns":{"amountInUsed":"  total uint input that will be transferred (kernel input + inFee)","inFee":"         swap-leg fee component included in amountInUsed (split LP / protocol)"}}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"burnAmounts(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Calculate the proportional withdrawal amounts for a given LP token amount."},"burnSwapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Calculate the amounts for a burn swap operation."},"denominators(address)":{"notice":"Per-token uint base denominators used to convert uint token amounts \u2194 internal Q64.64."},"fees(address)":{"notice":"Per-asset swap fees in ppm. For asset-to-asset swaps, the effective pair fee is the         sum of the two asset fees (each < 10,000 by constructor invariant)."},"fetchPoolState(address)":{"notice":"Bundle every field an off-chain quoter needs into one snapshot, so the         webapp / SDK can reconstruct the pool's pricing state from a single         RPC view call."},"maxLpForBudget(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Off-chain helper for budget-style callers: bisect to find the largest         lpAmountOut that can be minted within `maxAmountIn`. View-only \u2014 not for         on-chain consumption. Does not account for the \u03c3_swap gate or rate-limit         budget; callers should additionally call `quoteMint`/`quoteSwapMint` to         confirm the mint would land."},"mintAmounts(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Calculate the proportional deposit amounts required for a given LP token amount."},"poolPrice(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Price of one LP token denominated in `quoteToken` as Q64.64."},"price(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Infinitesimal marginal buy price for a swap input\u2192output as Q128.128,         denomination-adjusted to external token units."},"swapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Quote an exact-input swap. Mirrors the on-chain swap math."},"swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Closed-form exact-output swap quote (companion to `swapAmounts` exact-in)."},"swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Off-chain view helper: bisect to find the exact input amount that drives the forward         marginal buy price P_fwd(input\u2192output) up to `maxPrice` (denomination-adjusted)."},"swapMintAmounts(address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Quote an exact-LP-out swap-mint: given a target lpAmountOut, return the         gross input (kernel + swap fee) the pool will pull from the caller.         There is no protocol mint fee in this design."},"working(address)":{"notice":"Returns true iff the pool is not killed and has been initialized with liquidity."}},"version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/PartyInfo.sol":"PartyInfo"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":15000},"remappings":[":@abdk/=lib/abdk-libraries-solidity/",":@openzeppelin/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/",":abdk-libraries-solidity/=lib/abdk-libraries-solidity/",":ds-test/=lib/permit2/lib/solmate/lib/ds-test/src/",":erc4626-tests/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/erc4626-tests/",":forge-gas-snapshot/=lib/permit2/lib/forge-gas-snapshot/src/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/",":halmos-cheatcodes/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/halmos-cheatcodes/src/",":openzeppelin-contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/",":permit2/=lib/permit2/src/",":solmate/=lib/permit2/lib/solmate/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"lib/abdk-libraries-solidity/ABDKMath64x64.sol":{"keccak256":"0x1364fdc24192b982f647c7fc68dcb2f6fc1b5e201843e773144bd23a76cb3b97","license":"BSD-4-Clause","urls":["bzz-raw://490712cc07db32f274899b17aade9c975f06010848c21500b8a5ead6898e09c7","dweb:/ipfs/QmZMPKjDgwCFSGdLWJW6g5E7hDLByA9hNjXzAwJ4GKTZvN"]},"lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/interfaces/IERC1363.sol":{"keccak256":"0xd5ea07362ab630a6a3dee4285a74cf2377044ca2e4be472755ad64d7c5d4b69d","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://da5e832b40fc5c3145d3781e2e5fa60ac2052c9d08af7e300dc8ab80c4343100","dweb:/ipfs/QmTzf7N5ZUdh5raqtzbM11yexiUoLC9z3Ws632MCuycq1d"]},"lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/interfaces/IERC165.sol":{"keccak256":"0x0afcb7e740d1537b252cb2676f600465ce6938398569f09ba1b9ca240dde2dfc","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://1c299900ac4ec268d4570ecef0d697a3013cd11a6eb74e295ee3fbc945056037","dweb:/ipfs/Qmab9owJoxcA7vJT5XNayCMaUR1qxqj1NDzzisduwaJMcZ"]},"lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/interfaces/IERC20.sol":{"keccak256":"0x1a6221315ce0307746c2c4827c125d821ee796c74a676787762f4778671d4f44","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://1bb2332a7ee26dd0b0de9b7fe266749f54820c99ab6a3bcb6f7e6b751d47ee2d","dweb:/ipfs/QmcRWpaBeCYkhy68PR3B4AgD7as params.lpTokenAmount: The amount of LP tokens to burn

returns.withdrawAmounts: Array of token amounts that will be received

params.lpAmount: amount of LP tokens to burn
params.outputTokenIndex: index of target asset to receive

denominators(address).details: Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`.         Equivalent to `pool.denominators()` in the previous interface; moved here so that         PartyPool's deployed bytecode stays within EIP-170.

fees(address).details: Decodes the BFStore data contract pointed at by `pool.bfStore()` via `EXTCODECOPY`.

fetchPoolState(address).details: Same read-only-reentrancy caveat as the other quote helpers — do not use as      a same-transaction price oracle (see the interface-level NatSpec).

returns.amountInUsed: total uint input that will be transferred at that lpAmountOut
returns.lpAmountOut: largest feasible LP amount; 0 if budget is too small to mint anything

mintAmounts(address,uint256).details: Returns minimum token amounts (rounded up) to receive `lpTokenAmount` LP tokens      at current pool proportions. Returns zeros for the initial deposit (handled by      transferring tokens first, then calling `initialMint()`).

params.lpTokenAmount: The desired amount of LP tokens

returns.depositAmounts: Array of token amounts to deposit (rounded up)

poolPrice(address,uint256).details: Balanced approximation: pool value ≈ nAssets × quoteBalance.      Per-LP value = nAssets × quoteBalance / totalSupply / D[quote].

params.quoteTokenIndex: index of the quote asset in which to denominate the LP price

returns._0: price Q64.64 value equal to quote per LP token unit

price(address,uint256,uint256).details: Computed as `exp((q[input] − q[output]) / b) × D[input] / D[output]` where      `b = κ · effectiveSigmaQ` and `D[k] = denominators(pool)[k]`.      Anchored to the pool's `effectiveSigmaQ` (the block-aligned `min(σ_swap, σ_live)`      that swap()/swapMint()/burn() price against), so this is the *executable* marginal      price, not a live-Σq theoretical price. This is what makes the exact-price ceiling      workflow in `swapAmountsForExactPrice` safe: both share the same execution anchor.      Represents the cost in input token units to acquire one unit of the output token.      Fee-free and infinitesimal — actual cost for a finite swap will be higher.      On a balanced pool with equal denominators this returns exactly `1 << 128`.

params.inputTokenIndex: index of the token being sold
params.outputTokenIndex: index of the token being bought

returns._0: External buy price as Q128.128 uint256

params.maxAmountIn: exact input to transfer (fee is deducted from output, not added to input)

returns.amountOut: net output amount user would receive (gross output minus outFee)

swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint25.details: Given desired NET output `amountOut` (what the caller receives after fee), returns the      required input without iteration. Reverts if `amountOut` is infeasible.

params.amountOut: desired NET output in uint token units (after fee deduction)

returns.amountIn: total uint input required (no fee on input; fee is on output side)

swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256.details: Uses 64 iterations of bisection on the actual two-sided LMSR state. Returns      `(amountIn, minAmountOut, fee)` — pass both directly to `pool.swap()`.      NOT intended to be called from on-chain operations.      Caller workflow:        uint256 target = info.price(pool, i, j) * 1005 / 1000; // 0.5% slippage ceiling        (uint256 maxIn, uint256 minOut,) = info.swapAmountsForExactPrice(pool, i, j, target);        pool.swap(payer, sel, recv, i, j, maxIn, minOut, deadline, false, "");

params.maxPrice: Q128.128 denomination-adjusted ceiling on P_fwd.                         Must be strictly greater than info.price(pool, i, j).

returns.amountIn: exact input (uint token units; fee is on output side)
returns.amountOut: net output (uint token units); pass as `minAmountOut` to `swap()`

params.lpAmountOut: target LP shares the caller wants minted

returns.amountInUsed: total uint input that will be transferred (kernel input + inFee)
returns.inFee: swap-leg fee component included in amountInUsed (split LP / protocol)

burnAmounts(address,uint256).notice: Calculate the proportional withdrawal amounts for a given LP token amount.

burnSwapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Calculate the amounts for a burn swap operation.

denominators(address).notice: Per-token uint base denominators used to convert uint token amounts ↔ internal Q64.64.

fees(address).notice: Per-asset swap fees in ppm. For asset-to-asset swaps, the effective pair fee is the         sum of the two asset fees (each < 10,000 by constructor invariant).

fetchPoolState(address).notice: Bundle every field an off-chain quoter needs into one snapshot, so the         webapp / SDK can reconstruct the pool's pricing state from a single         RPC view call.

maxLpForBudget(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Off-chain helper for budget-style callers: bisect to find the largest         lpAmountOut that can be minted within `maxAmountIn`. View-only — not for         on-chain consumption. Does not account for the σ_swap gate or rate-limit         budget; callers should additionally call `quoteMint`/`quoteSwapMint` to         confirm the mint would land.

mintAmounts(address,uint256).notice: Calculate the proportional deposit amounts required for a given LP token amount.

poolPrice(address,uint256).notice: Price of one LP token denominated in `quoteToken` as Q64.64.

price(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Infinitesimal marginal buy price for a swap input→output as Q128.128,         denomination-adjusted to external token units.

swapAmounts(address,uint256,uint256,uint.notice: Quote an exact-input swap. Mirrors the on-chain swap math.

swapAmountsForExactOutput(address,uint25.notice: Closed-form exact-output swap quote (companion to `swapAmounts` exact-in).

swapAmountsForExactPrice(address,uint256.notice: Off-chain view helper: bisect to find the exact input amount that drives the forward         marginal buy price P_fwd(input→output) up to `maxPrice` (denomination-adjusted).

swapMintAmounts(address,uint256,uint256).notice: Quote an exact-LP-out swap-mint: given a target lpAmountOut, return the         gross input (kernel + swap fee) the pool will pull from the caller.         There is no protocol mint fee in this design.

working(address).notice: Returns true iff the pool is not killed and has been initialized with liquidity.
